g.text: A schema-pluggable, evidence-grounded agent for statistical annotation
one architecture · many labeling domains · verifiable annotation artifacts
TF-IDF + LR classifier · zero LLM tokens
reuses our trained ML baselines as a gate: difficulty = 1 − max_class_prob, plausible labels = top-k by prob
high-prob → fast track · low-prob / ambiguous → full jury · shortlists labels to halve downstream LLM cost
regex + ML + graph tools · LLM only at the gate
picks tools by a learned policy; favours non-LLM tools first (regex, embeddings, co-citation graph, ML classifiers)
↳ assembles a structured case file: rhetorical markers, section provenance, topic-sim score,
co-citation graph features, similar precedents from Case Library, ...
stop criterion: a small LLM gate says "evidence sufficient" — typically 0 or 1 LLM call
③ PROSECUTION PANEL (one advocate per shortlisted label)
attacks each advocate; a regex contradiction-pattern matcher pre-flags obvious holes before invoking the LLM critic
replaces blind multi-turn / CoT (which our experiments show often degrade accuracy)
⑤ FACT-CHECKER (NLI + exact-match · zero LLM tokens)
exact-string match → BLEU overlap → DeBERTa-NLI entailment, in that cheap-to-expensive order
hallucinated evidence dropped; LLMs deliberately forbidden — they cannot vouch for themselves
LLM weighs surviving arguments; non-LLM Platt scaler turns the raw margin into calibrated probability
tight margin → 2nd LLM family · emits { label, confidence, margin } · invokes abstain() if low
⑦ CLERK (pure code · no LLM) → produces the Annotation Artifact (see below)
LLM ENGINES · invoked only when above is unsure
domain tools declared in Schema Card —
ANNOTATION ARTIFACT (output) — this is what JUROR ships, not just a label
Why ship an artifact, not a label?
● every label has a tool-trace and verified spans
● rejected alternatives are explained, not silent
● low-confidence cases self-flag for human review
● artifacts feed Case Library → agent improves online
● replayable: same artifact reproduces same decision
